summary: Persistent memory system entirely removed — cross-session memory retrieval, LLM-based relevance matching, and end-of-session memory extraction are all deleted without replacement
failure_scenario: A user who previously configured preferred research topics, verified formula-paper dependencies, or feedback on recommendations loses all saved context. On every new session, the agent starts from zero with no recollection of prior work, preferences, or verified knowledge.

summary: Global JSONL audit logging removed — no persistent record of hook events (session lifecycle, mode changes, API calls, tool usage) is written to disk
failure_scenario: An operator debugging a production issue has no event trace to reconstruct what the system did and in what order. Session-creation, session-destruction, API-request, and step-mode-changed hook types are also deleted from HOOKS dict, so even in-memory tracking of these events is impossible.

summary: Background task system removed — all tools now run synchronously in the main agent loop; slow operations (large reads, long-running commands, subagent spawns) block the agent until completion
failure_scenario: A user requests a large file read (>5000 lines) or a subagent searches multiple papers. The entire agent loop blocks for the duration. Previously these were dispatched to background threads and results were collected on the next turn; now the agent is unresponsive until the operation finishes.

summary: System prompt reduced from 6 sections (base, guidelines, memory_rules, memory_index, skills, environment) to 1 section (guidelines) — agent loses awareness of available tools, current date, workspace, OS, and memory index
failure_scenario: The agent cannot determine the current date or its working directory from context, which may cause incorrect time-relative reasoning. The agent no longer knows what tools are available, reducing its ability to plan multi-step actions. The skill discovery system (SKILL.md scanning and load_skill tool) is also deleted.

summary: Cron scheduler removed — no mechanism for scheduled or recurring task execution; the schedule_cron, list_scheduled_jobs, cancel_scheduled_job tools are deleted
failure_scenario: A user who wants to set up a recurring task (e.g., 'check for new papers every hour', 'remind me about X tomorrow') has no way to do so. The agent loop injection (scheduler.set_agent_loop) and busy-flag coordination are also removed.

summary: api_request hook calls removed from both query() and followup() endpoints — no mechanism to observe or log API requests from external consumers
failure_scenario: An external Django/Flask application using the API bridge has no observability hook. Previously the api_request hook allowed audit logging, rate monitoring, or telemetry for every external query and followup call; now these calls are invisible to any hook handler.

summary: session_created and session_destroyed hook calls removed — session lifecycle is no longer observable via hooks
failure_scenario: Any hook handler that previously tracked active sessions (e.g., to enforce concurrent session limits, to clean up per-session resources, or for session-level audit trails) no longer fires. Sessions are created and destroyed silently.

summary: Git worktree isolation system removed — teammates no longer have isolated git branches/directories, and the task-to-worktree binding system is deleted
failure_scenario: When multiple teammates work on different tasks simultaneously, their file operations (reads, writes, git commands) collide in the same working directory. Previously each teammate had its own git worktree and branch, preventing cross-task interference. Now concurrent writes by different teammates can corrupt each other's state.
